title: turn labels in Dockerfile into OCI annotations

comments.body: Can you point me to examples?  All I can find from a quick search is command line flags, and `build` has had a `--annotation` flag for a while.
@ChristianKniep can you share the command you used with buildkit?

Buildkit does not. I used:
```
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM alpine:3.16.2
LABEL foo=bar
$ mkdir -p oci
$ docker buildx build -q . -o type=oci,dest=-,name=label/x86_64 | tar xf - -C oci
$ jq . oci/index.json
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "manifests": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
      "digest": "sha256:5fadbea4dfe878ac3234bec25193ad0235ebcbc997b9ebe8d9cac49913e5133e",
      "size": 506,
      "annotations": {
        "io.containerd.image.name": "docker.io/label/x86_64:latest",
        "org.opencontainers.image.created": "2023-01-10T15:50:56Z",
        "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name": "latest"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```
No `foo=bar` in sight..
@ChristianKniep, I must have misremembered.  I thought that you managed to do that in the demo you gave me last Friday?

Nope, sadly not.
But as you already said; it seems common sense that all Labels of an image should also populate the manifest annotations.

@vrothberg @ChristianKniep @nalind  Is this RFE still under consideration ? The idea SGTM but I can think of few edges cases like what would happen if two images with similar values are added to same manifest list ?

I'd take the `image.index` out of the equation here for the moment. FullAck on your concern and I do not think that that's an edge case even.
But if we only focus on a manifest - any concerns that labels within the cfg obj should not become annotations within the manifest?
For me, at least, it's not expected, and if we start checking conformance with BuildKit's OCI output the way we do with Docker-format output, we'll have to undo it to start getting tests to pass.

🤔 Ok, if it breaks tests we might not want to do that... I'll try to gather some more feedback from the HPC community is report back.
My suspicion is (the reason for bringing that up), that everyone expects when they do `LABEL` in a Dockerfile it will be visible in the manifest annotations. Not a false assumption IMHO. The manifest hash includes the config object and thus has the information tied to it anyways.

For a `manifest list`/`image index` I think that is not the case. The index might have a lowest common denominator kinda function. Say you have a GROMACS index and the index annotation state `application.name=gromacs` it should not be violated by all the manifest in the index. But that is an early idea - open for feedback on that.

I realize now that our current tests don't look at manifests, just rootfs contents and fields in image config blobs, so changing manifests wouldn't break future tests unless they started comparing manifests.  Given that different compression will cause blob digests to differ, I wouldn't add that if I ended up being the person writing those tests.

Annotations do get lost if we have to convert to Docker format.  This can happen when an image is being pushed, even if we end up converting it back to OCI format again at a later point, so personally I'd avoid depending on them to provide information that's meaningful at run-time.

Yupp. I am going to add all annotations as labels automagically, so that they are not lost.
I reckon that Labels are what a runtime can rely on, as they are carried by all formats. Annotations might come into play for higher level services like a scheduler or a registry.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think we can close this issue.  Please reopen/yell if I am mistaken.
